sub_problems: Define LLM-runtime analogs of 'notice' (logs of prior contamination), 'duty of inquiry' (provenance verification, sanitization checks), and 'value' (the cost of running the inquiry).
Implement a context-cleansing operator that takes a possibly-tainted piece of state plus a duty-of-inquiry artifact and returns clean state if the inquiry passed.
Evaluate that this mechanism breaks failure-propagation chains where binary trust labels fail.
